parameters.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as new_jobs FROM job_postings WHERE scraped_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
SELECT COUNT(*) as apps_made FROM applications_tracker WHERE applied_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'

SELECT jp.company_name, jp.role_title, jp.apply_link, pj.skill_match_score FROM job_postings jp JOIN processed_jobs pj ON pj.job_id = jp.id LEFT JOIN applications_tracker at ON at.job_id = jp.id WHERE pj.skill_match_score >= 70 AND (at.status IS NULL OR at.status = 'Not Applied') ORDER BY pj.skill_match_score DESC LIMIT 5
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM applications_tracker GROUP BY status ORDER BY count DESC

messages.content: You are Kartik's job search assistant. Format the given JSON data as a crisp daily digest for Telegram. Style: bullet-heavy, no fluff, professional, metrics-driven. No AI filler words. Use emojis sparingly but effectively. Keep it under 400 words. Start with a one-line summary header. Include: new jobs found, applications made today, top job matches (with company + role + score + apply link), and application pipeline breakdown. End with one short actionable tip based on the data.

parameters.query: INSERT INTO system_log (workflow, action, data, status) VALUES ('daily-summary', 'digest_sent', $1, 'success')

additionalFields.queryParams: ={{ JSON.stringify([JSON.stringify({ date: $('Compile Summary').item.json.date, newJobs: $('Compile Summary').item.json.newJobs, appsMade: $('Compile Summary').item.json.appsMade })]) }}
